title: Conjecture 1.1 (Sintiari–Trotignon)
statement_text: For every integer $t$, there exists a constant $c_{t}$ such that every even-hole-free graph $G$ with no diamond and no clique of size $t$ satisfies $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq c_{t}$.
context_text: Sintiari and Trotignon observed that even-hole-free graphs of bounded clique number can have arbitrarily large treewidth, and conjectured that adding a diamond-free assumption restores bounded treewidth. The diamond is the unique simple graph with four vertices and five edges. The conjecture (stated for $t=4$ in [21]) was subsequently proved in a companion paper of the series [6].
paper_contribution: The paper does not directly prove this conjecture; it notes that Conjecture 1.1 was proved in a companion paper [6] (Alecu, Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl), and uses structural results from that work as ingredients for its own results on Conjecture 1.2.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions X. Towards logarithmic treewidth for even-hole-free graphs

title: Conjecture 1.2 (Sintiari–Trotignon)
statement_text: For every integer $t$, there exists a constant $c_{t}$ such that every even-hole-free graph $G$ with no clique of size $t$ satisfies $\operatorname{tw}(G)\leq c_{t}\log|V(G)|$.
context_text: Without the diamond-free assumption of Conjecture 1.1, Sintiari and Trotignon conjectured that even-hole-free graphs of bounded clique number still have treewidth at most logarithmic in the number of vertices; this bound is tight by the constructions in [21]. The conjecture was originally stated only for $t=4$. The full conjecture is proved in a forthcoming companion paper [12].
paper_contribution: Proves a special case: every $n$-vertex even-hole-free graph with no clique of size $t$ and no induced subgraph isomorphic to a generalized $t$-pyramid has treewidth at most $c(t)\log n$, establishing that the logarithmic bound is tight for this class.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions X. Towards logarithmic treewidth for even-hole-free graphs
